name: Prefab Commercial Building
description:
Prefab commercial buildings give retailers, showroom owners and service businesses a finished, multi-room structure that opens for trade in weeks instead of the months a conventional build takes. SAMAN manufactures this as a configured 60′×36′×14′ unit — 2,160 sq ft of floor area, priced at ₹25,20,000 — built in our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities and installed on your site. This page is for three commercial buyers: the retailer who needs a customer-facing outlet, the showroom owner who wants a presentable storefront, and the service business setting up a branch or centre. If your requirement is a private office block or an industrial production unit, those are different builds — covered lower down so you land on the right one.

Retail outlets, showrooms and service centres: what a commercial-frontage build needs

A commercial prefab building is judged by its frontage, not only its floor plan. The structure faces customers, so the front elevation does more work than on any office or factory unit.

A prefab commercial building working as a street-facing retail outlet, with a glazed shopfront and signage band.

A retail prefab building usually wants a glazed shopfront for daylight and visibility, a clear customer entrance, and a signage- and branding-ready elevation the buyer can apply their identity to. A showroom needs an open, column-light interior so vehicles, furniture or appliances can be displayed without a structural grid breaking up the floor. A service centre — a branch with public reception, a sales desk and one or two work bays — needs a divided but welcoming public-access layout. We configure the same shell for all three by changing the openings, the partition positions and the external finish, which is why one base unit covers the bulk of commercial requirements.

The 60×36 ft commercial unit: size, steel-and-wood build, and what's configured

The standard SAMAN commercial unit measures 60′×36′×14′, giving 2,160 sq ft of usable floor and a 14 ft height that suits a display interior and signage above the entrance. It is built on a structural steel frame with insulated wall panels and a wood-based interior lining — the "Steel & Wood" specification — so the building is rigid enough for permanent commercial use yet light enough to transport and install whole.

The full 60×36 ft prefab commercial building in charcoal, showing the configured structure on a commercial plot.

At ₹25,20,000 the configured unit covers the steel structure, wall and roof panels, the framed door and window openings, and a commercial-grade external cladding ready for paint or branding. Flooring grade, glazing extent, partition count and the front-elevation treatment are specified to your use, and the footprint can be made larger or smaller where a site or trade demands it.

What does a prefab commercial building cost versus RCC?

A prefab commercial building like SAMAN's 2,160 sq ft unit carries a fixed factory price — ₹25,20,000, about ₹1,170 per sq ft for the configured structure. A comparable RCC commercial build usually costs more per square foot once glazing, services and several months of site labour are added, and it cannot open for trade until the wet work fully cures.

Commercial buildings also carry cost drivers a shed never does — more glazing, customer-grade HVAC, and fire provision — so the finished per-sq-ft figure rises with specification. The advantage of prefab is that the structural cost is fixed and known up front, and the building is trading while an RCC equivalent is still under scaffolding.

A completed prefab commercial building in beige, installed and ready to trade while RCC sites are still building.

Prefab commercial build RCC commercial build
Build time ~3–5 weeks, mostly off-site 4–7 months on-site
Civil work Level plinth only Full foundation, masonry, curing
Cost basis Fixed quote (from ₹25,20,000) Variable; rises with delays
Commercial frontage Glazing & signage configured in factory Added after structure is built
Relocation Can be lifted and moved Permanent — demolish to remove

Commercial outlet, office block or industrial unit: picking the right prefab building

"Prefab building" covers three commercial-scale formats, and choosing the wrong one costs you in layout. If your building is customer-facing — a shop, showroom or service centre — this commercial unit is the right one. If you need a private workplace built around cabins, partitions and desks rather than a sales floor, our prefab office buildings are configured for that work. For a factory or warehouse with an open production floor and no public frontage, a prefab industrial building suits better and is covered on its own page.

A navy prefab commercial building configured as a service centre, with public reception and a work bay.

And if you would rather see finished prefab structures and prices across formats side by side before deciding, you can compare our prefabricated buildings in one place.

Lead time, on-site installation and ordering your commercial build

A commercial unit this size is built and finished in the factory, then transported and installed on a prepared site. Because it ships as a 60 ft structure, the main planning point is site access for the delivery vehicle and a crane or equivalent to set the unit down; the base only needs a level plinth rather than a deep foundation.

A prefab commercial building being installed by crane onto a prepared plinth — the on-site delivery stage.

Once positioned, services are connected and the front elevation, signage backing and any internal partitions are handed over ready for your fit-out. This commercial building is one format within SAMAN's prefab building range, so if your project later needs an office block or an industrial unit alongside it, those can be ordered to match.

Frequently asked questions

How much does a prefab commercial building cost in India?
SAMAN's standard commercial unit is ₹25,20,000 for a 2,160 sq ft (60′×36′×14′) configured structure — roughly ₹1,170 per sq ft for the shell. The finished figure depends on glazing extent, flooring grade, HVAC and fire provision, which we quote against your specification.

What's the difference between a prefab commercial, office and industrial building?
A commercial building is customer-facing — a shop, showroom or service centre with frontage and public access. An office building is a private workplace of cabins and partitions. An industrial building is an open production or storage floor with no public frontage. Each is a separate SAMAN configuration.

Can a prefab commercial building be used for a customer-facing showroom or retail outlet?
Yes — that is what this unit is built for. The steel-framed structure is rated for permanent use, the front elevation is configured for glazing and signage, and the interior can be left open for display or partitioned for a reception and work bays.

What's included in the price of this prefab commercial building?
The ₹25,20,000 covers the configured structure: the steel frame, insulated wall and roof panels, framed door and window openings, and commercial-grade external cladding ready for finish. Flooring, glazing, partitions and fit-out are specified and quoted to your use.

Get a price for your commercial build

Tell us your trade, your site location and the frontage you need, and we'll quote the configured unit or a custom size.

Bangalore — Call +91 80886 85440 · WhatsApp +91 88616 22859
Delhi NCR — Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937
short_description:
60×36×14 ft commercial unit — opens for trade in weeks, not months.
Prefab commercial building for showrooms, retail outlets and service centres. Steel-and-wood structure with a signage-ready frontage, delivered and installed as a finished unit.
Size
60×36×14 ft (2,160 sq ft)
Capacity
Showroom, retail, branch
Frame
Structural steel
Panel
Insulated panel + wood
Price
₹25,20,000 (incl. taxes)
Delivery
Built in 3–5 weeks
Floor load
Commercial-grade floor
Relocations
Liftable & movable
SKU: SP-CC-40-2024
From ₹25,20,000
